segments.text: Ladies and gentlemen, please welcome to the stage, Diamond Leader, Johnny Nitalescu.
Thank you. I wish you remain with this energy till the end. Many of you have taken personal development forces, but it wasn't enough, right?
You've attended trainings, but it wasn't enough. And sometimes, not because they are not good, but because sometimes what you really need is for someone to change your perspective.
Or maybe just a story that leaves you speechless and shakes you out of your beliefs and limitations.
Today, I'm going to tell you a story. It's the story of my best friend. His name is not important right now, but his message is.
And I will come back to that message because it might change your life starting today.
Two years ago, in March, after a simple visit to the doctor because of a swelling in his neck, he received a diagnosis.
Cancer. Tone-silv cancer. He had a live nylon in 2015 with a suitcase full of dreams and a trolley full of plans for a better life.
And he worked for him. And he worked for him. He built. He was a man on his path. And suddenly, everything went silent.
Days and nights followed where life turned into continuous cycle of treatments.
and at lunch time a combination of radiation.
14 men started that journey in his therapy group.
shadows of the men they once were.
came a new reality for life.
a constant tremor in his neck,
and a deep loss of masculinity.
recognized the man in the mirror.
One day his wife told him,
We can't fight this battle alone.
She refused to accept the end.
To support him and rebuild his body
I don't want to leave me alone.
far beyond what he had been told
She was back in the race.
And he started to rebuild everything.
But you know what's worse than that.
Had become part of his DNA.
He was back in the hospital.
the driving test because they were no longer just a change of time.
They had to come with motivation to do more.
But his wife, his wife found something more.
Not just a purpose, but a community.
She found her path because her plan was pretty simple.
To see her husband well again.
But the universe had another plan for all of you to see her husband.
I want you now, please, to look at the screen.
Jim, my best friend, Jim's story is actually my story.
I have a good question for you.
When was the last time you asked your best friend, your reflection in the mirror, how he truly is?
Wait, hold on, hold on. This story actually is not about me.
I am just the voice. I am just the story.

Because this is about love. It is about decision. It is about loyalty. It is about trust. It is about sacrifice. It is about strategy. It is about friendship. It is about how many people look for you to say hello.
Not just on your birthday. It is about the team in front of me. Because...
You know something? Stories are always told by survivors. Never by the heroes who even make it. They become a man.
You know something? Stories are always told by survivors. Never by the heroes who even make it. They become a man.
And if I am standing here today, it is not because I was strong. It is because someone refused to give up on me. And after all, this is what is really about it.
